question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a massive number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via subscribeTopic() to exhaust the boundedElastic scheduler thread pool, causing legitimate subscribers' live message streams to stall and transactions to never be gossiped to them?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a startTime set to the minimum allowed value (0) and no endTime or limit, causing the historical retrieval phase via topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() to run indefinitely and block the live message pipeline, preventing new transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a non-existent topicId when checkTopicExists is false, causing subscribeTopic() to emit a synthetic Entity with type TOPIC and silently deliver an empty stream, effectively suppressing all gossip of real transactions for that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the safety check path in missingMessages() by subscribing with a startTime far in the past, causing repeated unthrottled gap-recovery queries that saturate the database connection pool and prevent other subscribers from receiving transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the duplicate-message suppression branch in missingMessages() where numMissingMessages <= -1 returns Flux.empty(), by crafting a scenario where a legitimate transaction is classified as a duplicate and silently dropped, preventing it from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a race condition in the non-atomic read-then-check pattern of isNext() combined with the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last field, where two concurrent messages arrive and the sequence check passes for both, causing onNext() to throw IllegalStateException and terminate the stream, dropping all subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user cause the IllegalStateException thrown in onNext() when out-of-order messages are detected to terminate the entire reactive Flux stream, resulting in all pending and future transactions being silently dropped for that subscriber without any retry?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set filter.getLimit() to 1 and subscribe repeatedly to a high-volume topic, causing the flux.take(1) to terminate after a single message and miss all subsequent transactions, effectively preventing gossip of the remaining messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a value just slightly after startTime (e.g., 1 nanosecond ahead), causing pastEndTime() to fire almost immediately via the RepeatSpec loop and terminate the live stream before any transactions are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a past timestamp so that isComplete() returns true immediately (filter.getEndTime() < startTime), causing incomingMessages() to return Flux.empty() and the safety check to be skipped, resulting in zero transactions being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user craft a filter where filter.getLimit() equals the current topicContext.getCount(), causing the computed limit in incomingMessages() to be 0, which is passed to topicListener.listen() and may cause the listener to return no messages, silently suppressing all future transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user flood the gRPC endpoint with subscribeTopic() calls to drive subscriberCount to a level that exhausts JVM heap via accumulated TopicContext objects and AtomicReference chains, causing OOM and crashing the service, preventing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the gap-recovery path in missingMessages() with a very large sequence gap (e.g., current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1 = Long.MAX_VALUE), causing numMissingMessages to overflow or generate an extremely large limit query that hangs the database and prevents transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the filter() predicate that checks t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber() to cause a legitimate transaction with a lower sequence number (e.g., due to reordering) to be permanently filtered out and never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set startTime to the current time minus 1 nanosecond and endTime to current time, causing the historical retrieval to return no messages and the live stream to immediately terminate via pastEndTime(), resulting in zero transactions being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the newFilter construction in incomingMessages() where startTime is set to last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1, and if last is a crafted or corrupted message with Long.MAX_VALUE timestamp, cause an arithmetic overflow that wraps startTime to Long.MIN_VALUE, skipping all real transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user cause the safety check branch (current == null) in missingMessages() to fire repeatedly by keeping the subscription alive without consuming messages, triggering repeated unthrottled retrieve() calls that exhaust database resources and starve other subscribers of transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to exactly startTime + 1 nanosecond so that isComplete() oscillates between true and false across the endTimeInterval boundary, causing the pastEndTime() Flux to terminate the stream at an unpredictable moment and drop in-flight transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L)) safety check by subscribing and immediately cancelling before the 1-second delay fires, causing a dangling Mono that holds a scheduler thread and accumulates over many requests to exhaust the boundedElastic pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the concatWith(Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live)) pipeline by sending a subscription that causes the safetyCheck Flux to emit messages that interleave with the live Flux in a way that confuses the sequence-number deduplication filter, causing real transactions to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user cause the newFilter built in missingMessages() to have endTime equal to current.getConsensusTimestamp() and startTime equal to last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1, where these are equal, resulting in an invalid time range that causes the retriever to return no messages and silently drop the gap transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set a very large limit value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) in the filter to cause integer overflow when computing remaining limit in incomingMessages() (filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get()), resulting in a negative limit passed to the listener that causes no messages to be fetched?
